2605.26029.title: CausaLab: A Scalable Environment for Interactive Causal Discovery Toward AI Scientists
2605.26029.paper_summary: We introduce CausaLab, a scalable environment for evaluating interactive causal discovery by LLM agents. Unlike prior evaluations, CausaLab evaluates both whether an agent can solve a problem using causal evidence and whether its answer is supported by a correct hypothesis about the underlying causal mechanism. Each episode places an agent in a synthetic laboratory: it receives prior measurement records, intervenes on a manipulator crystal, and predicts the resonance frequency of a held-out reactor crystal governed by the same mechanism. The hidden data-generating process is a randomly sampled structural causal model (SCM), so success requires recovering both a causal graph and structural equations rather than recalling prior knowledge. CausaLab also includes a domain-specific language that records the agent's evolving SCM hypothesis, making trajectories inspectable and comparable with ground truth. Experiments show a persistent gap between prediction and mechanism recovery: in the purely observational 6-node setting, GPT-5.2-high reaches 92% task accuracy but only 0.471 all-edge $F_1$. This observation further motivates our exploration of different interaction strategies: Mixed observation--intervention strategies improve structural fidelity: in the mixed 6-node setting, GPT-5.2-high achieves 80% on both task accuracy and all-edge $F_1$. Yet even strong agents struggle to design informative interventions, as pure intervention strategies perform poorly on both task accuracy and all-edge $F_1$. We identify premature stopping as a major weakness of agents, and show that asking the model to verify the consistency between its hypothesis and past data can help mitigate this issue. CausaLab therefore separates predictive success from causal understanding and exposes current LLM agents' limits as experimental causal reasoners.
2605.26029.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹 CausaLab，一個可擴展的環境，用於評估 LLM 代理的互動因果發現。與之前的評估不同，CausaLab 評估代理是否能夠利用因果證據解決問題，以及其答案是否受到關於潛在因果機制的正確假設的支持。每個實驗回合將代理置於一個合成實驗室：它接收先前的測量記錄，對一個操控晶體進行干預，並預測由相同機制控制的保留反應器晶體的共振頻率。隱藏的數據生成過程是一個隨機抽樣的結構因果模型 (SCM)，因此成功需要恢復因果圖和結構方程，而不是回憶先前的知識。CausaLab 還包括一種特定領域的語言，記錄代理不斷演變的 SCM 假設，使得軌跡可檢查並可與真實情況進行比較。實驗顯示預測與機制恢復之間存在持續的差距：在純觀察的 6 節點設置中，GPT-5.2-high 達到 92% 的任務準確率，但所有邊的 $F_1$ 只有 0.471。這一觀察進一步激勵我們探索不同的互動策略：混合觀察--干預策略提高了結構的保真度：在混合的 6 節點設置中，GPT-5.2-high 在任務準確率和所有邊的 $F_1$ 上均達到 80%。然而，即使是強大的代理在設計信息豐富的干預時也面臨困難，因為純干預策略在任務準確率和所有邊的 $F_1$ 上表現不佳。我們確定過早停止是代理的一個主要弱點，並展示要求模型驗證其假設與過去數據之間的一致性可以幫助減輕這一問題。因此，CausaLab 將預測成功與因果理解分開，並揭示當前 LLM 代理作為實驗因果推理者的局限性。
2605.26029.author: Junlin Yang et.al.
2605.26029.authors: Junlin Yang, Dylan Zhang, Xiangchen Song, Qirun Dai, Xiao Liu, Yuen Chen, Aniket Vashishtha, Jing Shi, Chenhao Tan, Hao Peng
